evals.prompt: I have a Node.js Express API project with user and order modules. I want to set up a proper test suite for it — API tests and unit tests. Can you help me build the testing system?
evals.expected_output: Agent scans the project, generates a Q&A HTML page for user to select test types/modules/environment/data strategy, then proceeds to design and implement tests covering all 6 mandatory test points per selected type.
expectations: Agent performs project analysis identifying tech stack and modules
Agent generates an interactive Q&A HTML page
Q&A page includes test type selection, module coverage matrix, environment config, data strategy
Agent produces a structured JSON config from Q&A page output
Agent designs a test plan covering all 6 mandatory test points for each selected test type
Agent implements test files organized by type
Agent runs quality verification using run-all-checks.sh
Agent invokes a sub-agent for deep quality review, not self-review

evals.prompt: I just built a Python Flask app with three endpoints and I have zero tests. Help me create a comprehensive test suite from scratch. I want API tests and integration tests. I'm on a Mac, using pytest, and I want to test against my local dev database.
evals.expected_output: Agent follows the full workflow: analysis → Q&A page → test plan → implementation → quality check. Tests cover CRUD, auth, boundary cases, status codes, data validation, logs, and error handling for both API and integration test types.
expectations: Agent detects Python/pytest stack correctly
Test plan covers both API and integration test types
Each test type includes all 6 mandatory test points
Integration tests cover database read/write consistency
Quality scripts detect the pytest adapter
Sub-agent review is invoked with adversarial prompt

evals.prompt: My Go project already has some unit tests but coverage is low and I suspect some tests are flaky. Can you evaluate what I have and improve the test suite? Focus on unit and integration testing.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs existing tests, evaluates quality using run-all-checks.sh, identifies gaps, generates a Q&A page to confirm scope with user, then fills in missing coverage following the 6 mandatory test points.
expectations: Agent runs existing tests and reports current state
Agent uses run-all-checks.sh to measure coverage, stability, performance
Agent identifies which of the 6 test points are missing per module
Agent generates Q&A page for user to confirm scope
New tests fill gaps without duplicating existing coverage
Sub-agent reviews both new and existing tests
